heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Robert Wise. Written by Ernest Lehman, based on the novel by Howard Lindsay and Russel Crouse, which was based on an autobiography by Maria von Trapp (The von Trapp Family Singers) and the 1959 musical of the same name by Rodgers and Hammerstein.
clean: Directed by Robert Wise. Written by Ernest Lehman, based on the novel by Howard Lindsay and Russel Crouse, which was based on an autobiography by Maria von Trapp (The von Trapp Family Singers) and the 1959 musical of the same name by Rodgers and Hammerstein.

heading: Maria Rainer-Von Trapp
raw: [singing] Raindrops on roses and whiskers on kittens,Bright copper kettles and warm woolen mittens,Brown paper packages tied up with strings,These are a few of my favorite things.
clean: Raindrops on roses and whiskers on kittens,Bright copper kettles and warm woolen mittens,Brown paper packages tied up with strings,These are a few of my favorite things.

heading: Reverend Mother

clean: How do you solve a problem like Maria?How do you catch a cloud and pin it down?Many a thing you know you'd like to tell her, many a thing she ought to understand...How do you hold a moonbeam in your hand?

heading: Reverend Mother

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maria: I just couldn't help myself. The gates were open and the hills were beckoning and everything was so green and fresh, and the Untersberg kept leading me higher and higher, as if it wanted me to go right through the clouds with it.
clean: I just couldn't help myself. The gates were open and the hills were beckoning and everything was so green and fresh, and the Untersberg kept leading me higher and higher, as if it wanted me to go right through the clouds with it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reverend Mother: Child, suppose darkness had come and you were lost?
clean: Child, suppose darkness had come and you were lost?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reverend Mother: Some people would call that honesty.
clean: Some people would call that honesty.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maria: Oh, but it's terrible, Reverend Mother. You know how Sister Berthe always makes me kiss the floor after we've had a disagreement? Well, lately I've taken to kissing the floor whenever I see her coming, just to save time.
clean: Oh, but it's terrible, Reverend Mother. You know how Sister Berthe always makes me kiss the floor after we've had a disagreement? Well, lately I've taken to kissing the floor whenever I see her coming, just to save time.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Captain: You are the twelfth in a long line of governesses who have come here to look after my children since their mother died. I trust you will be an improvement on the last one. She stayed only two hours.
clean: You are the twelfth in a long line of governesses who have come here to look after my children since their mother died. I trust you will be an improvement on the last one. She stayed only two hours.

clean: What's wrong with the children, Captain?

heading: Dialogue

clean: Now, when I want you, this is what you will hear.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maria: Oh, no, sir, I'm sorry, sir. I could never answer to a whistle. Whistles are for dogs and cats and other animals, but not for children, and definitely not for me. It would be too... humiliating.
clean: Oh, no, sir, I'm sorry, sir. I could never answer to a whistle. Whistles are for dogs and cats and other animals, but not for children, and definitely not for me. It would be too... humiliating.

raw: Maria: Oh, much more, sir!

raw: Captain: Um, what gift?

heading: Dialogue

clean: Uh-huh. Then I suggest that you keep it, and let us eat.

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue
